pattern: Count arithmetic subsequences by tracking, for each index and common difference, how many weak (length two or more) subsequences end there; extending each such run by a later matching element promotes it to a full counted slice.
hints: Think about subsequences that share a common difference and end at a particular index, even the short ones of length two.

For each pair (j, i) with i after j, the difference fixes a run: the number of length-two-plus subsequences ending at j with that difference can all be extended through i, and each extension that already had length two becomes a valid slice.

Maintain dp[i] mapping a difference to the count of subsequences of length two or more ending at i; for each j<i with d=nums[i]-nums[j], add dp[j][d] to the answer (those are length-three-plus) and fold dp[j][d]+1 into dp[i][d].

edge_inputs.description: evenly spaced values give several nested slices
all equal values, difference zero, many slices
fewer than three elements cannot form a slice
no three elements share a difference
large magnitudes whose differences exceed 32 bits

stress.description: small value range over a moderate length so many subsequences share a difference
stress.note: narrow range maximises collisions on a common difference, exercising the dp counts heavily
